title: Jedi Temple dormitory
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Cleanup}} {{Location |image=[[File:Jedi_Temple_accommodation_sector_AoI.jpg]] |name=Jedi Temple dormitory |created= *Rebuilt c. [[35 ABY/Legends|35 ABY]]''[[Fate of the Jedi: Outcast]]'' |destroyed=*[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]{{Film|III}} *c.[[137 ABY]][[Legacy (2006) 14|''Legacy'' (2006) 14]] |type= |location=[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] *[[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]]''[[Star Wars: Jedi Council: Acts of War]]'' *Rebuilt in the [[New Jedi Temple]] |terrain= |fauna= |flora= |interest= |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]][[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']] *[[New Jedi Order]] }} {{Quote|I shall confer with Master Yoda. He will put your mind at rest, I'm sure. For today, though, no more training. Return to your dormitory.|[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Tera Sinube/Legends|Tera Sinube]], to [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]]|{{SWComicCite|7.9|Temple of Phantoms}}}} The '''dormitory''' of the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] was an area of the ancient edifice that contained the [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|youngling]] nurseries, the [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] dormitories, the [[Knights' Billet]], and the chambers of the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]]. Additionally the area was equipped with a visitors' section to accommodate diplomats waiting to confer with the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]]. ==History== {{Quote|Find the young ones first—kill ''all'' the Jedi children!''"
"''Commander, the sleeping quarters are empty!|A [[Yinchorri/Legends|Yinchorri]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]] and a Yinchorri warrior|[[Jedi Council: Acts of War 2|''Jedi Council: Acts of War'' 2]]}} Erected within the Jedi Temple over its multiple expansions since the Temple's initial construction in [[3996 BBY]], the accommodation sector was completed in its modern form as of [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]]. In [[88 BBY/Legends|88.5 BBY]], Padawans [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] and [[Lorian Nod]] infiltrated the private room of Master [[Thame Cerulian]] in search of anything that would give Dooku a hint in to the mind of his new master. While perusing Cerulian's selection of [[Holobook/Legends|holobooks]], Dooku discovered a text revealing the process used to access the [[Dark Holocron]] stored within the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Temple Library]]. Later, after news of the [[Holocron/Legends|holocron's]] disappearance, Dooku rushed To Nod's room where he found the young man suffering from the effects of accessing the [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifact]]. Entering soon on the heels of Dooku was High Council member [[Oppo Rancisis/Legends|Oppo Rancisis]] who confiscated the artifact and instructed the two to wait for the High Council to summon them.''[[Legacy of the Jedi]]'' At the time of the [[Incident at the Jedi Temple|series of robberies]] plaguing the Temple, Masters [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Tahl]] were assigned by the High Council to oversee the investigation. Meeting Tahl in her quarters, Jinn met her new assistant [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[2JTJ|TooJay]] that was presented to the Knight after she lost her eyesight during the [[Mission to Melida/Daan]]. During the investigation, [[Qui-Gon's quarters/Legends|Qui-Gon's quarters]] were broken into by [[Bruck Chun]], an apprentice working with the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Xanatos]]. While he didn't take anything, Tahl was able to detect a distinct odor in Jinn's room which she was able to trace back to the Temple [[lake level]].''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Uncertain Path]]'' Later in Tahl's chambers, Jinn and his Padawan, [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] met with Padawan [[Siri Tachi/Legends|Siri Tachi]] regarding her knowledge of Chun's past. Jinn voiced his suspicion of Xanatos working with Chun, a suggestion that made Tahl weary. After Xanatos revealed himself, the Temple's energy systems started to fluctuate. The senior students' wing was suffering from extreme heat and had to be flushed by Knight [[Miro Daroon]]. Because of the threat to the students at the Temple, Jinn and Tahl ordered that they all move to the heart of the Temple and set up temporary dormitories. Soon after Jinn confronted Xanatos and was successful in driving him from the Temple. A short time later the dormitories reopened and the students returned to their rooms.''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Captive Temple]]'' After the death of Tahl, Jinn would wander the halls of the Temple during the early hours of the morning, only returning to his quarters to have Kenobi bring him [[Sapir tea|sapir]] [[Tea/Legends|tea]]. On one such night, as it neared dawn, Master Yoda confronted Jinn and warned him of his disability to let go. Jinn took this simple scolding and the late night walks ended.[[Secrets of the Jedi (novel)|''Secrets of the Jedi'']] [[File:Jedi_Temple_accommodation_sector_AoW.jpg|thumb|200px|left|Yinchorri warriors in the accommodation sector during their raid on the Jedi Temple]] During the [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]] [[Yinchorri Uprising]], a group of [[Yinchorri warrior caste|Yinchorri warriors]] [[Raid on the Jedi Temple (Yinchorri Uprising)|infiltrated]] the Jedi Temple in accordance to the mysterious [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious's]] plan. After entering the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|atrium]] of the [[High Council Tower/Legends|High Council Tower]], the Yinchorri rushed through the Temple and were met with no resistance. Searching the accommodations of the youngest Jedi in an attempt to wipe out the Order's next generation of warriors, the Yinchorri invaders were surprised to find them abandoned. Returning to the atrium they were ambushed by the Jedi Masters present in the Temple and all of the intruders were defeated. In Master [[Anoon Bondara]]'s quarters, Padawan [[Darsha Assant]] explained how she had failed her [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Jedi Trials]] in the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|Coruscant underworld]]. Assigned to recover the [[Fondorian]] [[Oolth]], Assant had allowed him to fall to his death. Not believing he was dead, Master Bondara accompanied Assant to check the area for his body. On this mission, Bondara was attacked and killed by [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]], the secret [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]] of the elusive Darth Sidious.''[[Darth Maul: Shadow Hunter]]'' Amidst the battles of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Temple life remained charged with lightsaber practice and military planning. Many diplomats and envoys were constantly moving in and out of the visitor's accommodations, having clearance to use the public areas of the Temple without a Jedi escort or guard. During the war, [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]] escorted an armored [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] into the Temple at the behest of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]]. Claiming he had vital information for the [[Office of the Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Office of the Supreme Chancellor]], Fett refused to reveal his identity as he wished to make a second assassination attempt on [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], the man who had killed his [[Jango Fett/Legends|father]]. Sneaking out of his temporary room, Fett quickly made his way to the Council Members' level only to find [[Mace Windu's quarters|Windu's quarters]] empty.''[[Boba Fett: Pursuit]]'' Within the final months of the war, Padawan [[Whie Malreaux/Legends|Whie Malreaux]] was stationed at the Temple with his master, [[Maks Leem]]. He was plagued by night terrors while he slept in the Temple's dormitories; within the dreams he saw a [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|dark woman]] forcing him to kiss a wounded [[Tallisibeth Enwandung-Esterhazy|girl]], and later his death at the hands of a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]-wielding [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|horror]].''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'' The Clone Wars ended with Master Windu discovering the truth, Darth Sidious and Chancellor Palpatine were one and the same. Moving to [[Showdown on Coruscant|arrest]] the Sith, Windu was killed by Jedi-turned-traitor Anakin Skywalker, now Darth Vader. In a move to catch the Jedi off their guard, Sidious instructed Vader to take the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] to [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|seize]] the Temple and slaughter its occupants. While the senior Padawans and Knights were armed, many of the younglings hid in their rooms waiting for the raid to end. However, the Jedi protecting the dormitories were cut down and the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] raced in and shot the children in their beds. Shortly after the Temple was occupied by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], ex-Jedi [[Ferus Olin]] infiltrated the Temple in an effort to find the rumored [[Storage floor|Jedi Prison]]. While using a service corridor from one of the Council spires, Olin went in the direction of the accommodation sector in order to position himself closer to the [[Room of a Thousand Fountains/Legends|Room of a Thousand Fountains]] several levels below. While in the area, Olin and Flume noted that the accommodation sector wasn't as heavily damaged as the rest of the Temple.''[[The Last of the Jedi: Underworld]]'' When Sidious and Vader were slain, the Empire lost balance and was eventually [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|ousted]] from Coruscant. During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] Coruscant was devastated by [[Terraforming/Legends|terraforming]], causing the Temple to crumble.''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand]]'' Following the reclaiming of the world, the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] [[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chief of State]], [[Cal Omas]], ordered the [[Reconstruction Authority]] to rebuild the ancient edifice as a gift for the great sacrifices made by the Order against the [[Yuuzhan Vong]].''[[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King]]'' Rebuilt as it had been several decades before, the accommodation sector began to be filled with Padawans and Knights as the [[New Jedi Order|restored Jedi Order]] began to grow. In an area known as the [[Knights' Billet]], Grand Master [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] summoned Knights [[Tahiri Veila]], [[Tesar Sebatyne]], and [[Lowbacca]] to discuss their relationship with the nests of the [[Killik/Legends|Killiks]]. Deeming them to be [[Joiner]]s and telling them that their judgment was skewed because of it, Skywalker exiled them to [[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]] for meditation and reflection.''[[Dark Nest III: The Swarm War]]'' ==Layout and style== {{Quote|Is… is it gone? Did I save the younglings?''"
"''''Younglings''? What are you talking about? They're not even out of bed yet.|[[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]|}} [[File:Jedi_Temple_accommodation_sector_ToP.jpg|thumb|250px|right|[[Tiplee/Legends|Tiplee]] and Anakin Skywalker in the accommodation sector]] Located within the [[First Knowledge Quarter/Legends|northwest]] corner of the Temple, the accommodation sector occupied the floors directly below the Jedi Archives and Library. Separated into several different levels, the sector was made up of nurseries, youngling dormitories, the Knights' Billet, private rooms for the senior Knights and Masters, temporary residences for Knights in between assignments, and visitors' quarters for diplomats and politicians visiting the Temple. Like the rest of the Temple, the rooms had little furniture, providing only that which was necessary, and were pillared. Overseen by older Jedi, the nurseries of the Temple were used to hold the youngest of the Initiates brought to Coruscant by the [[Acquisition Division/Legends|Acquisition Division]]. With age, the younglings would be sorted into clans and placed into dormitories which were monitored by a clan leader. The dormitories of the accommodation sector were large enough to accommodate an entire clan of initiates, each overseen by a Jedi Knight or Master. Once the Initiates were chosen to become Padawans they moved into slightly more private chambers within the dorms but were all on central hallway. These chambers were equipped with a small cot and a desk. All rooms were modeled to maintain an atmosphere suitable to the different species of Jedi within the Order. Padawan [[Bant Eerin/Legends|Bant Eerin]] was known to have a moist, humid atmosphere in her room at all times due to her [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] biology. Young Knights were placed in the Knights Billet, a more spacious dorm environment which had a common room, snack annex, and lightsaber training dojo. After years of active duty, Knights who were regularly based out of the Temple would be afforded their own private quarters which had connections to the Archives and Library databases. Like the chambers of the Masters, the rooms had closets and bookcases disguised by slide away panels. Each room contained a sleep couch, a sofa, a workbench and a table with chairs. Aside from the permanent residences, temporary living quarters were assigned to Jedi who took on long journey missions; during the Clone Wars, Jedi such as Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker would be assigned these quarters as they were on constant assignment during the war. Like the other accommodations, the rooms were maintained by [[Temple employee]]s and [[Cleaning droid/Legends|cleaning droids]].
